Basic Computer Skills Test The Basic Computer Skills Test evaluates a candidate's knowledge of fundamental computer skills, including data entry, Linux, Excel, computer programming aptitude, shell scripting, typing, system administration, and data analysis. Covered skills: Basic Computer Skills Data Entry Excel Typing System Administration Test Duration ~ 40 mins Difficulty Level Moderate Questions 15 Computer Skills MCQs Availability Available as custom test Get started for free Preview questions About the Basic Computer Skills Assessment Test The Basic Computer Skills Test helps recruiters and hiring managers identify qualified candidates from a pool of resumes, and helps in taking objective hiring decisions. It reduces the administrative overhead of interviewing too many candidates and saves time by filtering out unqualified candidates at the first step of the hiring process. The test screens for the following skills that hiring managers look for in candidates: Efficient data entry and accuracy Proficient in using Excel formulas and functions Strong typing skills with good accuracy Knowledge of basic computer hardware and software Able to troubleshoot common system administration issues Understanding of file management and organization Ability to navigate and utilize software applications efficiently Attention to detail in data processing and analysis Ability to follow instructions for performing tasks Familiarity with basic networking concepts Efficient time management and task prioritization Proficiency in using keyboard shortcuts and hotkeys 1200+ customers in 80 countries Use Adaface tests trusted by recruitment teams globally. Adaface skill assessments measure on-the-job skills of candidates, providing employers with an accurate tool for screening potential hires. Get started for free Preview questions Non-googleable questions We have a very high focus on the quality of questions that test for on-the-job skills. Every question is non-googleable and we have a very high bar for the level of subject matter experts we onboard to create these questions. We have crawlers to check if any of the questions are leaked online. If/ when a question gets leaked, we get an alert. We change the question for you & let you know. How we design questions These are just a small sample from our library of 15,000+ questions. The actual questions on this Basic Computer Skills Test will be non-googleable. 🧐 Question Easy Basic Network Troubleshooting Network settings Connectivity issues Networking Troubleshooting Solve You are unable to connect to the internet. All cables are properly connected and your Wi-Fi shows as connected with strong signal. What should you first check to resolve this issue? A: Check if the router is turned on B: Restart your computer C: Check the DNS settings D: Replace the Ethernet cable E: Check your firewall settings F: Flush the DNS cache Easy Identifying Malware Symptoms Operating systems Troubleshooting Error Handling Solve Your computer starts to run extremely slowly, there are unexpected ads popping up even when you're not browsing the internet, and your friends report receiving strange emails from your account. Which of the following is the most likely cause of these issues? A: The hard drive is full B: The CPU is overheating C: You have installed conflicting software D: Your computer is infected with malware E: Your internet connection is slow F: There are too many background applications running Easy Understanding File Extensions Operating Systems File Management File Knowledge File Type Identification Solve File extensions help the system understand what type of file it is dealing with, and consequently, which applications can be used to open it. Given a list of file extensions, identify the one that typically represents a compressed file format. 🧐 Question 🔧 Skill Easy Basic Network Troubleshooting Network settings Connectivity issues Networking Troubleshooting 2 mins Computer Skills Solve You are unable to connect to the internet. Adaface are very responsive and friendly to deal with. Kirsty Wood, Human Resources, WillyWeather We were able to close 106 positions in a record time of 45 days! Adaface enables us to conduct aptitude and psychometric assessments seamlessly. My hiring managers have never been happier with the quality of candidates shortlisted. Amit Kataria, CHRO, Hanu We evaluated several of their competitors and found Adaface to be the most compelling. Great library of questions that are designed to test for fit rather than memorization of algorithms. Swayam Narain, CTO, Affable Why you should use Pre-employment Basic Computer Skills Test? The Basic Computer Skills Test makes use of scenario-based questions to test for on-the-job skills as opposed to theoretical knowledge, ensuring that candidates who do well on this screening test have the relavant skills. The questions are designed to covered following on-the-job aspects: Basic computer skills like file management and troubleshooting Data entry accuracy and speed Knowledge of Excel functions and formulas Typing speed and accuracy System administration tasks like user management and software installations Basic understanding of computer hardware Familiarity with operating systems and software applications Ability to troubleshoot and resolve software and hardware issues Knowledge of data organization and retrieval techniques Understanding of basic networking concepts Once the test is sent to a candidate, the candidate receives a link in email to take the test. For each candidate, you will receive a detailed report with skills breakdown and benchmarks to shortlist the top candidates from your pool. What topics are covered in the Basic Computer Skills Test? Basic Computer Skills: Basic Computer Skills refer to the fundamental knowledge and proficiencies required to operate a computer system effectively. This includes understanding concepts like file management, internet browsing, and navigating the operating system. Measuring this skill ensures that candidates have the necessary foundation to perform tasks using a computer. Data Entry: Data Entry entails accurately and efficiently inputting data into a computer system or database. It involves skills such as keyboarding, attention to detail, and familiarity with software applications used for data entry. Assessing this skill is important to identify candidates who are capable of handling large volumes of data with precision. Excel: Excel proficiency involves the ability to effectively use Microsoft Excel, a powerful spreadsheet software, for tasks such as data analysis, calculations, and creating reports. This skill measures a candidate's knowledge and expertise in using functions, formulas, and features in Excel to manipulate and organize data, making them more efficient in handling complex data-driven tasks. Typing: Typing skill refers to the ability to accurately and quickly input text on a keyboard. This includes touch typing, proper finger placement, and familiarity with the layout of the keyboard. Measuring this skill provides insight into a candidate's typing speed and accuracy, which is crucial in many roles that require extensive typing such as data entry, transcription, and administrative work. System Administration: System Administration involves managing and maintaining computer systems, including hardware, software, networks, and user accounts. This skill includes tasks like installing and updating software, troubleshooting technical issues, and ensuring system security. Measuring this skill helps identify candidates who possess the technical knowledge and abilities required to effectively manage computer systems in an organization. Full list of covered topics The actual topics of the questions in the final test will depend on your job description and requirements. However, here's a list of topics you can expect the questions for Basic Computer Skills Test to be based on. File management Data entry Excel formulas Keyboard shortcuts Troubleshooting Computer hardware Software applications Data processing Task prioritization System administration Networking concepts Accuracy and precision Excel functions Typing speed File organization Data analysis Following instructions Basic computer skills Keyboard skills Efficiency in software usage Network troubleshooting Data integrity Excel formatting Error handling Time management Software installation Computer peripherals Data validation File compression Internet browsing Keyboard layout System backup and recovery Data privacy and security Excel charts and graphs Typing accuracy Software updates Computer maintenance Excel sorting and filtering Data storage devices System updates Network protocols Data migration Keyboard ergonomics Excel pivot tables Typing techniques Software customization Computer virus prevention Data manipulation System optimization Network configuration Data backup Keyboard function keys Excel conditional formatting Typing practice Software licensing Computer troubleshooting Data visualization System documentation What roles can I use the Basic Computer Skills Test for? Data Entry Operator Linux Administrator Excel Specialist Computer Programmer Shell Scripter Data Analyst Windows System Administrator How is the Basic Computer Skills Test customized for senior candidates? For intermediate/ experienced candidates, we customize the assessment questions to include advanced topics and increase the difficulty level of the questions. This might include adding questions on topics like Proficiency in database management systems Ability to configure and maintain computer networks Knowledge of advanced Excel functions like PivotTables and macros Proficiency in data analysis and reporting Ability to create and manage spreadsheets Experience with data entry software and tools In-depth understanding of computer security and data protection measures Knowledge of system backup and recovery procedures Experience with virtualization technologies Ability to perform system performance optimization Preview this test View sample scorecard Try the most advanced candidate assessment platform AI Cheating Detection with Honestly ChatGPT Protection Non-googleable Questions Web Proctoring IP Proctoring Webcam Proctoring MCQ Questions Coding Questions Typing Questions Personality Questions Custom Questions Ready-to-use Tests Custom Tests Custom Branding Bulk Invites Public Links ATS Integrations Multiple Question Sets Custom API integrations Role-based Access Priority Support GDPR Compliance Screen candidates in 3 easy steps Pick a test from over 500+ tests The Adaface test library features 500+ tests to enable you to test candidates on all popular skills- everything from programming languages, software frameworks, devops, logical reasoning, abstract reasoning, critical thinking, fluid intelligence, content marketing, talent acquisition, customer service, accounting, product management, sales and more. Invite your candidates with 2-clicks You can invite candidates by adding their email address with 2 simple clicks or send them a public link to complete the test. To invite candidates in bulk, you can add a list of email addresses or upload a CSV file. You can also edit the invite email template to match your employer brand. Make informed hiring decisions As soon as a candidate completes the test, you receive an email notification and a link to their report. The report analyses their performance on a question-level and category-level to give you a sense of their strengths and weaknesses. The report comes with an overall pass/ fail recommendation and industry benchmarks to help you make an informed decision. Get started for free Preview questions Have questions about the Basic Computer Skills Hiring Test? What is Basic Computer Skills Test? The Basic Computer Skills Test assesses a candidate’s proficiency in fundamental computer skills. Employers use it to evaluate potential hires for roles requiring basic computer literacy. Can I combine Basic Computer Skills Test with Excel questions? Yes, recruiters can request a custom test with multiple skills. For assessing Excel skills, you can refer to our Excel Test to understand the type of questions included. What skills are evaluated in the Basic Computer Skills Test? This test evaluates skills in Basic Computer Skills, Data Entry, Excel, Typing, and System Administration. For senior roles, it also includes database management, computer networks, advanced Excel, data analysis, security measures, and virtualization technologies. How to use Basic Computer Skills Test in my hiring process? You can use this test before or after resume screening, or as a resume replacement. Purchase the test, send it to candidates, and receive immediate results to make informed hiring decisions. What are the main computer skills tests? Other important computer skills tests include: Computer Literacy Test Data Entry Test Excel Test System Administration Online Test Can I combine multiple skills into one custom assessment? Yes, absolutely. Custom assessments are set up based on your job description, and will include questions on all must-have skills you specify. Here's a quick guide on how you can request a custom test. Do you have any anti-cheating or proctoring features in place? We have the following anti-cheating features in place: Hidden AI Tools Detection with Honestly Non-googleable questions IP proctoring Screen proctoring Web proctoring Webcam proctoring Plagiarism detection Secure browser Copy paste protection Read more about the proctoring features. How do I interpret test scores? The primary thing to keep in mind is that an assessment is an elimination tool, not a selection tool. A skills assessment is optimized to help you eliminate candidates who are not technically qualified for the role, it is not optimized to help you find the best candidate for the role. So the ideal way to use an assessment is to decide a threshold score (typically 55%, we help you benchmark) and invite all candidates who score above the threshold for the next rounds of interview. What experience level can I use this test for? Each Adaface assessment is customized to your job description/ ideal candidate persona (our subject matter experts will pick the right questions for your assessment from our library of 10000+ questions). This assessment can be customized for any experience level. Does every candidate get the same questions? Yes, it makes it much easier for you to compare candidates. Options for MCQ questions and the order of questions are randomized. We have anti-cheating/ proctoring features in place. In our enterprise plan, we also have the option to create multiple versions of the same assessment with questions of similar difficulty levels. I'm a candidate. Can I try a practice test? No. Unfortunately, we do not support practice tests at the moment. However, you can use our sample questions for practice. What is the cost of using this test? You can check out our pricing plans. Can I get a free trial? Yes, you can sign up for free and preview this test. I just moved to a paid plan. How can I request a custom assessment? Here is a quick guide on how to request a custom assessment on Adaface.
